title: Emission (signal + statistic, the L1 canonical shape)
description: One envelope for everything that happens in the lab. Bus semantics (signal) and lake semantics (statistic) are the same instance: every emission fans out to scoped subscribers AND lands durably in the lake. The semantic layer (L2) is auto-derived from these. AUTHORIZATION (contract §4.3): the server validates `source`, `type` namespace, `scope`, and max `severity` against the authenticated producer's emit capability — none of these are trusted from the body alone. Size bounds: whole envelope <= 16 KiB; <= 24 dimensions; <= 24 measures; <= 16 links. This is a server-accepted shape; consumers of the bus/lake MUST ignore unknown fields.

id.description: Idempotency key: a duplicate /emit returns the ORIGINAL {seq} (idempotent accept). Native producers mint random v4; bridges MUST mint deterministic v5 ids (namespace = source path/table, name = cursor/content hash) so a restarted bridge cannot double-land history.

type.description: Dot-namespaced event/statistic name (e.g. doorman.link.flap, host.cpu.pct). The semantic-layer registry key. Auto-registration of a NEW type is gated per producer (rate-capped, quarantined on flood/hostile names — surfaced as a curation proposal). Reserved namespaces (audit.*, attention.*, governance.*, task.*, card.*, console.api.*, delivery.*, bridge.*) accept only console-api-internal and trusted system producers.

ts.description: When it happened (producer clock, RFC 3339 UTC). The lake additionally stamps received_at server-side; freshness computations use received_at when |ts - received_at| exceeds the skew threshold, and a clock.skew emission flags the producer.

service.description: Emitting service. VALIDATED against the producer's identity: a system:control-plane token may emit source.service control-plane; agent:janet may not (source_mismatch, fail-loud).

host.description: Canonical .N host label, mc34, or VPS.

subject.description: What the emission is about — an entity key, joinable. Canonical formats: agent handle (janet), host (.14), service (host/service), task id (task:4711), card id (card:<uuid>), item id (item:<id>). For audit.op emissions subject IS the target entity (pinned — per-target audit derivations depend on it).

subject_kind.description: Entity kind of subject — the node type edges attach to. Optional but strongly encouraged; bridges always stamp it.

severity.description: First-class severity. p0 cracks the facade; interrupt tier is reserved for p0/safety/principal-command. Producers are capped at their capability's max severity (agents cannot emit p0 into scopes they don't own). NOTE: dispatcher-priority bridging maps priority 0 -> p0 for SEVERITY display only; interrupt ELIGIBILITY stays the dispatcher's LOCKED interrupt_policy domain (/task/709) — the two never merge.

scope.description: ReBAC visibility tag. REQUIRED and AUTHORIZED: a producer may emit only into scopes its capability grants (own agent:<self>; fleet if granted; user:*/restricted:* only with an explicit emitter grant). Un-scoped or unauthorized-scope emissions are rejected fail-loud. FLAT model: fleet does not imply agent:janet; visibility is exactly the grant set. Multi-audience events pick ONE scope; bridges use the narrowest scope that serves the surfaces bound to that type.

dimensions.description: Low-cardinality facets (string/bool; booleans allowed). Comms emissions pin these dimension names: method, in_reply_to, recipient, requires_reply, thread (contract §4.4).

measures.description: Numeric values. Timestamp-valued measures ride as epoch-seconds numbers with meta.fields.<name>.kind = timestamp.

rel.description: Edge relation (runs_on, deployed_to, preceded, supervises, belongs_to, ...).

links.description: Edges baked at ingest (graphing design §04): producer/bridge-asserted relationships materialized with the statistic attached, so 'why?' is later a graph walk (reserved GET /api/v1/graph), not a re-query.

body_ref.description: Reference to a lake-stored blob for payloads too large/free-form for dimensions (comms bodies, rpc response output tails). Blob reads are scope-checked like the emission. NEVER a secret-bearing payload: the emit path applies a secret scrubber (claim_token, Authorization, token-shaped strings) and rejects on match.

kind.description: Aggregation honesty: sum over a gauge and avg over a counter are both wrong; the structured query validator and the rollup jobs consult this.

fields.description: PER-FIELD typing hints keyed by dimension/measure name — the Phase-0 statistic-contract requirement that lets L2 auto-derive without guessing. Optional; absent fields fall back to observed inference.
